mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum; higher growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because corporate revenues track household spending.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth above zero are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point rise in UNRATE over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) captures stagflationary supply shocks that squeeze corporate margins and compress valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition (INDPRO down month-over-month and CPIAUCSL up month-over-month) does not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than the sum of independent marginal effects (interaction term t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL forces upward revisions to the expected terminal federal funds rate, raising equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day S&P 500 return during tightening cycles with month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) transmits immediately into S&P 500 prices within a 5-day window because labor market strength signals near-term consumer spending capacity and reduces recession probability.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above the prior 3-month average by more than 50 thousand units signals accelerating residential investment that multiplies broad demand for materials and labor, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the trigger are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in UNRATE and increase in PAYEMS indicates a tightening labor market with wage pressure that lifts nominal revenues but compresses margins; the net effect is negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to immediate margin repricing.
failure_condition: The joint condition (UNRATE down and PAYEMS up month-over-month) does not produce significantly negative 5-day returns compared to the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2% signals aggregate demand expansion that supports upward corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 releases indicating >2% annualized growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
